exams.name: JLPT
exams.note: Knowledge-based test. Since 2010 the JLPT publishes no official word/kanji lists — the counts below are widely used community estimates. You must clear both the overall pass mark and every sectional minimum.

levels.label: Basic
canDo: Read hiragana, katakana, and basic kanji expressions
Use greetings and common set phrases

levels.label: Elementary
canDo: Read familiar daily-topic passages in basic vocabulary and kanji

levels.label: Intermediate
canDo: Read concrete everyday materials and grasp headline information
Follow coherent conversation at near-natural speed

levels.label: Upper Intermediate
canDo: Read articles, commentaries, and simple critiques on varied topics
Understand news reports and natural-speed conversation, grasping intent

levels.label: Advanced
canDo: Read logically complex, abstract writing and grasp structure and intent
Follow lectures, news, and natural-speed conversation in detail

exams.name: JFT-Basic
exams.fullName: Japan Foundation Test for Basic Japanese
exams.note: Task / can-do based CBT (~50 questions, 60 min), targeting CEFR A2. Accepted as the language requirement for the Specified Skilled Worker (特定技能) visa. The Japan Foundation's free Irodori (いろどり) courseware is the aligned A1–A2 curriculum.

levels.label: Basic — Life in Japan
canDo: Introduce yourself and talk about daily routine
Shop and order at restaurants (prices, allergies)
